description: Append-only, hash-chained audit log capturing every state-changing action for regulatory defensibility and forensic investigation

append_only.description: MUST: No UPDATE or DELETE permitted on audit entries via any code path. Enforced at DB permission level (REVOKE UPDATE, DELETE from application role).

hash_chain.chain_formula: entry_hash = sha256(entry_id || actor_id || action || entity_type || entity_id || occurred_at || prev_hash || canonical_json(before_state) || canonical_json(after_state))
hash_chain.genesis_hash_description: First entry in chain uses a genesis hash of 64 zero hex characters as prev_hash

privacy.pii_handling: MUST: Before/after payloads containing PII are encrypted with envelope encryption; the hash is computed over plaintext then payload is sealed at rest
privacy.right_to_erasure: MUST: POPIA right-to-erasure against a data subject erases only the PII envelope contents (cryptographic shredding of per-record key); the hash chain remains intact to preserve audit integrity
privacy.access_logging: SHOULD: Every read of the audit log is itself logged

successful_append.description: Happy-path append: caller is authorized, chain tip is fresh, entry hashes and persists atomically
given: prev_hash matches the most recent entry_hash (or genesis_hash if chain is empty)

then.description: Append the entry with computed entry_hash

successful_append.result: Entry persisted; chain extended by one link

chain_mismatch.description: Concurrent-append race or tamper attempt — provided prev_hash doesn't match the current chain tip
given: prev_hash does not match current chain tip (concurrent append race or tamper attempt)
chain_mismatch.result: Append rejected; caller must retry with current chain tip. If mismatch persists outside of normal concurrency, triggers security alert.

integrity_scan_failed.description: Daily verification recomputed an entry hash and it no longer matches — evidence of tampering or storage corruption
given: daily integrity scan detected an entry whose stored entry_hash does not match a recomputed hash

then.description: Security alert with affected entry_id range

integrity_scan_failed.result: Alert raised; forensic investigation initiated. Audit log remains readable but chain is flagged.

query_served.description: Authorized auditor runs a filtered read; the read itself is logged for meta-audit
given: caller has auditor role or equivalent compliance permission

given.description: Filter by actor_id, entity_id, entity_type, action, or date range

query_served.result: Filtered, paginated audit entries returned with their entry_hash values for independent verification

unauthorized_read.description: Caller lacks auditor/compliance role — deny and log the denied attempt
given: caller lacks auditor or compliance role
unauthorized_read.result: 403 returned; read attempt itself is audited

unauthorized_write.description: Caller is not a whitelisted service account — only backend services may append entries
given: caller is not a whitelisted service account
unauthorized_write.result: 403 returned; write attempt itself is audited

errors.message: Audit log chain tip has advanced. Please retry.
Audit log integrity check failed. Security team has been notified.
You do not have permission to read the audit log.
Only authorized services may write to the audit log.

events.name: audit.entry_appended
events.description: A new audit entry was appended to the hash-chained log

events.name: audit.chain_mismatch
events.description: A write attempt referenced a stale chain tip

events.name: audit.integrity_violation_detected
events.description: Integrity scan found an entry whose hash does not match

anti_patterns.rule: Do not allow UPDATE or DELETE at the application layer even for administrators
anti_patterns.why: An audit log that can be edited by the people it audits is not an audit log. Enforcement must be at DB grant level.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not skip hash computation on bulk imports or migrations
anti_patterns.why: Unhashed entries break chain verification and create plausible-deniability gaps an attacker can exploit.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not store PII in plaintext in before/after payloads
anti_patterns.why: Audit logs accumulate indefinitely and are often exported to auditors; plaintext PII violates POPIA data minimization and transborder rules.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not hash over non-canonical JSON
anti_patterns.why: Key order and whitespace differences will produce different hashes for equivalent payloads, breaking verification. Use RFC 8785 JCS.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not use a truncated or non-cryptographic hash (CRC32, MD5, SHA-1)
anti_patterns.why: Collision resistance is required to make tampering infeasible. SHA-256 minimum.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not couple audit writes to the same transaction as business writes without careful ordering
anti_patterns.why: If the audit write fails silently after the business write commits, the log has a gap. Use outbox pattern or two-phase commit.

anti_patterns.rule: Do not expose raw database access to auditors
anti_patterns.why: Direct DB access bypasses read-audit logging. Auditors read through an API that itself emits audit.query_executed events.

related.reason: Audit entries containing PII must satisfy POPIA minimization, retention, and right-to-erasure obligations
Append-only storage and DB-level REVOKE grants are implemented in the persistence layer
Audit log query endpoints should be rate-limited to prevent auditor-credential abuse

goals.description: Maintain a cryptographically verifiable record of every state change for regulatory defensibility and forensic investigation

success_metrics.measurement: Percentage of daily integrity scans that verify the entire chain without mismatch
99th percentile time from action to durable audit entry

constraints.description: No code path may UPDATE or DELETE audit entries
PII in payloads must be encrypted; right-to-erasure applied via cryptographic shredding only

human_checkpoints: on integrity violation detection — requires human forensic review before declaring benign

invariants: for every entry e_n where n > 0: sha256(canonical(e_n)) == e_n.entry_hash
for every entry e_n where n > 0: e_n.prev_hash == e_{n-1}.entry_hash
audit entries are never modified after creation
PII fields are never logged in plaintext outside the encrypted payload
